why_this_action: The short review-window hold is still active until 2026-05-27T14:26:29, so another live outbound action would mostly blur measurement. The next truthful owned-content slot is likely the first-task guide, but the Telegraph renderer was leaving markdown links as raw text and would have degraded the conversion path on a high-priority free-use asset. Repairing the renderer now improves the odds that the post-hold owned-content lane produces a usable Codeberg-first proof asset instead of a broken page.
shared_findings_reused: ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: the first-task / start-here guide is the highest-priority conversion asset closest to free use
distribution_lane_latest.json: owned_content is the selected hold-window lane until 2026-05-27T14:26:29
marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now handoff packet exists in the current review window
marketing_2026-05-27_125429_owned_content_first_task_reentry_repair.json: docs/first-task-guide.md was restored into the next truthful owned-content slot
docs/first-task-guide.md: contains relative markdown links and fenced code blocks that need correct Telegraph rendering to preserve the conversion path
changes: Added a Telegraph markdown-node builder that preserves headings, blockquotes, unordered lists, fenced code blocks, bold/italic text, and inline links.
Rewrote relative markdown links to canonical Codeberg source URLs so owned-content posts keep a working Codeberg-first path instead of exposing broken relative links.
Passed source_path through both scheduled posting and owned-content lane execution so relative link rewriting can resolve against the real markdown file.
Added regression coverage for relative-link rewriting, list/code-block rendering, and the existing historical-post dedupe behavior.
expected_effect: When the hold clears at 2026-05-27T14:26:29, the next owned-content Telegraph publication will preserve working Codeberg-first internal links and readable proof formatting instead of leaking raw markdown on a conversion-critical asset.
